msgs.text:
I treated the prompt and prior log as a proposal record, not as proof. The prompt fixes the tame-format submission: distinguished sigma,tau, implicit tau^sigma=tau^2, later generators in the pro-2 core, and exponents reduced modulo M=85667662080.The prior log still leaves only the dynamic tame-inertia candidate unrefuted; the scalar, plain-π2, and fixed-τ−2 variants have recorded finite-quotient failures.

The live candidate remains:variables sigma,tau,x,y definitions nx = (x*tau^50932630783)^40491355905 ny = (y*tau)^40491355905 relations x*sigma^45176306175*x*sigma^40491355905*x^-1*nx*ny^sigma*y^-1

Equivalently, with π=π2 and η=2π on odd tame inertia,r=xσ−πxσπx−1(xτ−η)π ((yτ)π)σy−1,where π≡40491355905,−π≡45176306175,−η≡50932630783(modM).The stable equations from the prior turns should be kept as equations, not informal quotients:(1−tαa)Da=1−(tαa)π=1−t,soDa=(1−t)(1−tαa)−1.

Here t is the sign-cover involution and τ↦α=1 is a nontrivial odd tame-inertia character. This makes Da radical and −1+D−η a unit, so nontrivial odd tame-character blocks contribute only unit Smith pivots.

For the trivial tame-inertia block, the safe convention is(1+T−s)X+(1+T)Y=0,s≡π(modf),and Ps (T) is defined by (1+T)Ps (T)=1+T−s.

Then Y=Ps (T)X+Yˉ, and the reduced multiplier h(T)=1+Ps (T)+Ps(T−1)has no nontrivial f-th-root zero, since (1+ζ)h(ζ)=ζ−s+ζs+1=ζ−s(1+ζ2s+1),and 2s+1 is coprime to f. With these conventions, the prior abelianization conclusions remain coherent:Ue,fab≃Z2ef+1⊕C2,(kerκ)ab≃Z22ef+1⊕C4.My new contribution is to turn Turn 70’s primitive-residual test into a canonical normal form, not just a vanishing criterion.Letc=i=1∏rℓiai be odd, and identify

Z/cZ≃i∏

Z/ℓiaiZ.

Write a coordinate in the i-th factor asui+ℓiai−1vi,ui∈Z/ℓiai−1Z,vi∈Z/ℓiZ.

For a coefficient function r:Z/cZ→F2, defineru,vprim=ε∈{0,1}r∑r(u1+ℓ1a1−1ε1v1,…,ur+ℓrar−1εrvr),where every vi=0.Then the primitive exact-order c residual vanishes if and only if all these ru,vprim vanish. More strongly, after subtracting lower-inflation terms, there is a canonical representative supported on the positions where every top digit vi=0, and its coefficients are exactly the ru,vprim. The number of these coefficients isi∏ℓiai−1(ℓi−1)=φ(c),so this gives a minimal coefficient basis for the primitive residual quotient.

Examples:For c=9, write indices as a+3u, a,u∈Z/3. The primitive-9 coordinates arera+3u+ra,a∈Z/3, u∈(Z/3)×.Thus primitive 9 vanishes exactly when the three blocks {a,a+3,a+6} are constant.For c=pq squarefree, choosing anchor 0 in both coordinates gives the minimal rectangle coordinatesru,v+ru,0+r0,v+r0,0,u∈(Z/p)×, v∈(Z/q)×.This is equivalent to all 2×2 rectangle sums vanishing, but uses exactly (p−1)(q−1)=φ(pq) independent tests.For c=99=9⋅11, write the 9-coordinate as a+3u, a,u∈Z/3, and the 11-coordinate as v. The primitive-99 coordinates arera+3u,v+ra,v+ra+3u,0+ra,0,for a∈Z/3, u=0, and v=0. This is the first genuinely mixed prime-power/squarefree pattern.I also computed the verifier-modulus-supported anti-cyclotomic schedule. LetModd=32 ⋅5⋅7⋅11⋅13⋅17⋅19⋅23.

Among odd divisors c∣Modd, a primitive first-IA anti-cyclotomic test exists exactly when some n satisfies 2n ≡−1(modc). Sorted by minimal tame degree 2cn(c), the visible primitive tests are:c	least n with 2n=−1	minimal f=2n	degree cf	φ(c)3	1	2	6	2 5	2	4	20	4 9	3	6	54	6 11	5	10	110	10 17	4	8	136	16 13	6	12	156	12 33	5	10	330	20 19	9	18	342	18 65	6	12	780	48 57	9	18	1026	36 99	15	30	2970	60 171	9	18	3078	108 209	45	90	18810	180 627	45	90	56430	360 1881	45	90	169290	1080 Thus, for verifier-supported odd tame orders, no primitive first-IA test involving 7 or 23 appears: ord7 (2)=3 and ord23 (2)=11 are odd, so 2n ≡−1 is impossible modulo those primes. In the unrestricted mathematical proof, c=27 and c=25 still enter before c=43 and c=65; they are just not supported by the odd part of this verifier modulus.

ESTABLISHED: The dynamic candidate remains the only unrefuted candidate. I preserved the stable equationsDa=(1−t)(1−tαa)−1,(1+T)Ps (T)=1+T−s.New this turn: I gave a canonical primitive-residual normal form. For c=∏ℓiai, after subtracting lower inflations, the primitive exact-order c coefficients are the mixed top-digit sums ru,vprim, giving exactly φ(c) independent tests. I also computed the verifier-supported anti-cyclotomic priority schedule.

GAP: No actual arithmetic-minus-candidate residual has been computed. The presentation is still not proved. The missing step remains either a dyadic formation theorem or explicit Kummer/Witt arithmetic showing that the arithmetic filtered tame action has zero residual in every primitive anti-cyclotomic component.

NEXT: Compute normalized arithmetic-minus-candidate residuals for c=3,5,9,11. For c=9, use the primitive normal coefficients ra+3u+ra, then descend to c=3. For composite tests such as c=33,65,57,99, use the canonical top-digit normal form rather than cyclotomic division or basis-dependent projections.[BREAKTHROUGH]
